examples.text: وَدَخَلَ الْمَدِينَةَ عَلَىٰ حِينِ غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ أَهْلِهَا فَوَجَدَ فِيهَا رَجُلَيْنِ يَقْتَتِلَانِ هَـٰذَا مِن شِيعَتِهِ وَهَـٰذَا مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ ۖ فَاسْتَغَاثَهُ الَّذِي مِن شِيعَتِهِ عَلَى الَّذِي مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ فَوَكَزَهُ مُوسَىٰ فَقَضَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ۖ قَالَ هَـٰذَا مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ ۖ إِنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ مُّضِلٌّ مُّبِينٌ
examples.english: [One day] he entered the city at a time when its people were not likely to take notice. He found there two men fighting, this one from among his followers, and that one from his enemies. The one who was from his followers sought his help against him who was from his enemies. So Moses hit him with his fist, whereupon he expired. He said, ‘This is of Satan’s doing. Indeed he is clearly a misguiding enemy.’

etymology.text: Compare شَاعَ (šāʕa, “to spread (intransitive), to become known”). Cognate with Hebrew סיעה (si'a, “party, faction”).

body.text: this is the primary signification: so called from their agreeing together, and following one another: or, accord. to some, the
, which means “ he collected his people or party: ”
(S, O, Msb, K) of a man: (S, O, K:)
any people that have combined in,
not all of them agreeing together:
of a man: (O, TA:) [for] the word is applied to
(K, TA,) without variation: (TA:) the pl. is
, (S, * O, Msb, K,) the latter a pl. pl.; (Msb;) and the former is applied to
case, is one, who follow one another's opinion.
(S.) The saying, in the Kur [xxxiv. last verse],
As was done with the likes of them, of the same persuasion as they, of the peoples that have gone before:
(S, * TA:) and similar to this is the saying in the Kur liv. 51. (TA.) ―         -b2-  Afterwards,
all who took as their friends,
lords, 'Alee and the people of his house:
those who followed 'Alee, saying that he was the
Imám after the Apostle of God, and believing that the office of Imám should not depart from him and his descendants:
an innumerable people, who are innovators; the extravagant zealots among them are the Imámeeyek, who revile the Two Sheykhs
and the most extravagant of them call the Two Sheykhs disbelievers: some of them rise to the pitch of
A single person of this party,
agreeably with what has been said above; and such a person is likewise called ↓

entries.body_html: الشيعة: هم الذين شايعوا عليًّا، رضي الله عنه، قالوا: إنه الإمام بعد رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم، واعتقدوا أن الإمامة لا تخرج عنه وعن أولاده.
